text: Supervised learning is a common method in machine learning. First, we collect a set of training data, which contains examples with known answers. For instance, if we want a system to recognize cats, we show it many pictures labeled “cat” or “not cat.” The machine then learns patterns from this data to build a model. Once trained, the model can make predictions about new, unseen data. Finally, we measure its accuracy by comparing the predictions to the correct answers. A model with high accuracy is reliable and can be used in real-world applications like email filtering or medical diagnosis.
